Inkar_data_wrangling

Finn_Krüger

2023-11-09

Loading data

pequiv

SOEP <- read.csv('/Users/finnkruger/Documents/GitHub/Master_thesis_MDS/Data/SOEP/cs-transfer/CSV/soepdata/pequiv.csv', header = TRUE, sep = ",") %>%
  filter(syear > 2011)

SOEP <- SOEP %>%
  select(
pid, 
syear, 
alg2,
d11101, # Age
d11102ll, # Sex of Individual
d11108, # Education with Respect to Highschool
d11109, # Number of Years of Education
e11102, # Employment Status of Individual
 iunby, # UNEMPLOYMENT INCOME
e11103, # Employment Level (part-time/ vs full)
e11105_v1, # ISCO88
e11105_v2, # ISCO08
e11106, # Digit Industry Code 1of Individual
e11107, # Digit Industry Code 2 of Individual
i11101, # Household pre Government
i11102, # Household post Government
i11104, # Household Asset Income
i11103, # household labour income 
i11110, #Labor Earnings of Individual
y11101, # Consumer price index baseline 2015
l11101, # Federal State
l11102, # Region
p11101, # Life Satisfaction 
starts_with('w111') # Weights https://www.diw.de/documents/publikationen
) 


SOEP <- SOEP %>%
  rename(
    Age = d11101,
    Sex = d11102ll,
    Education_Highschool = d11108,
    Education = d11109,
    Employment_Status = e11102,
    Unemployment_Income = iunby,
    Employment_Level = e11103,
    Industry_Digit_1 = e11106, 
    Industry_Digit_2 =  e11107,
    ISCO88 = e11105_v1,
    ISCO08 = e11105_v2, 
    Household_Pre_Government = i11101,
    Household_Post_Government = i11102,
    Household_Asset_Income = i11104,
    Household_Labour_Income = i11103,
    Labor_Earnings = i11110,
    Consumer_Price_Index = y11101,
    Federal_State = l11101,
    Region = l11102,
    Life_Satisfaction = p11101
  )

SOEP Core, variables of interest

### Pulling out Climate Change Concern, Party Support, # Party Support


# 2012
Variables_2012 <- read.csv('/Users/finnkruger/Documents/GitHub/Master_thesis_MDS/Data/SOEP/cs-transfer/CSV/soepdata/raw/bcp.csv', header = TRUE, sep = ",") %>%
  select(pid, syear,
         bcp12706, # Climate Change worry
         bcp12601, # Party Support
         bcp12602, # Party support strength
         bcp124,    # Interested in Politics
         bcp08 # Unemployed
  ) %>%
  rename(
    CCC = bcp12706,
    Party_support = bcp12601,
    Party_strength = bcp12602,
    political_interest = bcp124,
    Unemployed = bcp08, 
  )


# 2013
Variables_2013 <- read.csv('/Users/finnkruger/Documents/GitHub/Master_thesis_MDS/Data/SOEP/cs-transfer/CSV/soepdata/raw/bdp.csv', header = TRUE, sep = ",") %>%
  select(pid, syear,
         bdp13307, # Climate Change worry
         bdp13201, # Party Support
         bdp13202, # Party support strength
         bdp130,    # Interested in Politics
         bdp15 # Unemployed
  ) %>%
  rename(
    CCC = bdp13307,
    Party_support = bdp13201,
    Party_strength = bdp13202,
    political_interest = bdp130, # Renaming to political_interest
    Unemployed = bdp15
  )

Variables_2021 <- read.csv('/Users/finnkruger/Documents/GitHub/Master_thesis_MDS/Data/SOEP/cs-transfer/CSV/soepdata/raw/blp.csv', header = TRUE, sep = ",") %>%
  select(pid, syear,

#Variables_2021_check <- read.csv('/Users/finnkruger/Documents/GitHub/Master_thesis_MDS/Data/SOEP/cs-transfer/CSV/soepdata/raw/blp.csv', header = TRUE, sep = ",") 

###### Making the dataframe

variable_names <- rbind(paste0("Variables_", 2012:2021))

dfs <- mget(variable_names)

names(dfs) <- NULL


### Filtering out not included in this version, creating AFD Dummies, creating CCC Dummies
combined_df_variables <- do.call(rbind, dfs)  %>%
  mutate(CCC_Binary =
           case_when(
             CCC == 3 ~ 1,
             TRUE ~ 0
           ),
         AFD_Binary = 
           case_when(
             Party_support == 27 ~ 1,
             TRUE ~ 0
           ),
         Unemployed = 
           case_when(
             Unemployed == 2 ~ 0,
             TRUE ~ Unemployed
           )
  )

Satisfaction with democracy

# 2016
Satisfaction_2016 <- read.csv('/Users/finnkruger/Documents/GitHub/Master_thesis_MDS/Data/SOEP/cs-transfer/CSV/soepdata/raw/bgp.csv', header = TRUE, sep = ",") %>%
  select(pid, syear,
         bgp0112
  ) %>%
  rename(
    Satisfaction_democracy = bgp0112
  )


# 2020
Satisfaction_2020 <- read.csv('/Users/finnkruger/Documents/GitHub/Master_thesis_MDS/Data/SOEP/cs-transfer/CSV/soepdata/raw/bkp.csv', header = TRUE, sep = ",") %>%
  select(pid, syear,
         bkp_01_11
  ) %>%
  rename(
    Satisfaction_democracy = bkp_01_11
  )

# 2021

Satisfaction_2021 <- read.csv('/Users/finnkruger/Documents/GitHub/Master_thesis_MDS/Data/SOEP/cs-transfer/CSV/soepdata/raw/blp.csv', header = TRUE, sep = ",") %>%
  select(pid, syear,
         blp_04_11
  ) %>%
  rename(
    Satisfaction_democracy = blp_04_11
  )



Satisfaction_democracy <- rbind(Satisfaction_2016,Satisfaction_2020,Satisfaction_2021)

Political Ideology

### Political Ideology

Ideology_2014 <- read.csv('/Users/finnkruger/Documents/GitHub/Master_thesis_MDS/Data/SOEP/cs-transfer/CSV/soepdata/raw/bep.csv', header = TRUE, sep = ",") %>%
  select(pid, syear,
         bep122 # Political Ideology
  )%>%
  rename(
    Political_Ideology = bep122
  )

Ideology_2019 <- read.csv('/Users/finnkruger/Documents/GitHub/Master_thesis_MDS/Data/SOEP/cs-transfer/CSV/soepdata/raw/bjp.csv', header = TRUE, sep = ",") %>%
  select(pid, syear,
         bjp_171 # Politcal Ideology 
  )%>%
  rename(
    Political_Ideology = bjp_171
  )

Political_Ideology <- rbind(Ideology_2014,Ideology_2019)

Additional Variables 2021 - Trust in Parliament & Frequency Media Usage

### Trust in Parliament 2021 

Additional_variables_2021  <- read.csv('/Users/finnkruger/Documents/GitHub/Master_thesis_MDS/Data/SOEP/cs-transfer/CSV/soepdata/raw/blp.csv', header = TRUE, sep = ",") %>%
  select(pid, syear,
         blp_163_01, 
         blp_10_03
         # what about blp_04_11 Satisfaction with democracy
         #blp_04_05: Satisfaction With Household Incom
         
  ) %>%
  rename(
    Trust_parliament = blp_163_01,
    Social_Media = blp_10_03
  )

Merging Dataframes and Filtering out appropriate NAs for BASIC Model

SOEP_final <- combined_df_variables %>%
  left_join(Political_Ideology, by = c("pid", "syear"))%>% 
  left_join(Satisfaction_democracy, by = c("pid", "syear"))%>%
  left_join(Additional_variables_2021, by = c("pid", "syear"))%>% 
  inner_join(SOEP, by = c("pid", "syear"))

## First filtering

SOEP_final <- SOEP_final %>%
  filter(!(Age < 16)) %>% # Age # 3drop
  filter(!(Sex == -1)) %>% # Sex # nothing
  filter(!(Education < 0))  %>%  # Education 15-16k ### 150/ 4000k AFD VOTES
  filter(!(CCC == -5)) %>% # Climate Concern 40k 
  filter(!(Party_support == -5))%>%  # Party ID
  filter(!(Household_Post_Government < 0.001))  %>% # Post Government Income 
  filter(!(Unemployed < 0)) %>%# Unemployed
  filter(Federal_State > 0)

Transforming Variables of interest

### What about Income --- What about Unemployment

#Household income vs labor income ( you might be from a rich household but earn a shitty wage and vote AFD)
# One way to control for this… how much your income contributes to household income. ? you might be an invidiual that is from a rich household, with no income and do not vote AFD)…. The average income of people who vote for afd is higher than those who do not…

## Calculating an individuals share of income from total household income


SOEP_final <- SOEP_final %>%
  mutate(Income_share_of_HH =  (Labor_Earnings + 0.0001) / (Household_Labour_Income+0.0001),
         Real_Income = (Household_Post_Government / Consumer_Price_Index)*100 ,
         Logged_HH_Income = log(Real_Income), 
         Sex =  # Mutate Sex in to Binary 0 Female and 1 for Male
           case_when(
             Sex == 1 ~ 1,
             TRUE ~ 0),
         Yt_1_unemployed =  # Mutate Sex in to Binary 0 Female and 1 for Male
           case_when(
             Unemployment_Income > 0 ~ 1,
             TRUE ~ 0 )
  )



# Create a new variable with decile ranks for every year
SOEP_final <- SOEP_final %>%
  group_by(syear) %>%
  mutate(decile = ntile(Real_Income, 10))


# Calculate cutoff points for each decile per year
cutoff_points <- SOEP_final %>%
  group_by(decile, syear) %>%
  summarise(min_value = min(Real_Income),
            max_value = max(Real_Income))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'decile'. You can override using the
## `.groups` argument.
print(cutoff_points)
## # A tibble: 100 × 4
## # Groups:   decile [10]
##    decile syear min_value max_value
##     <int> <int>     <dbl>     <dbl>
##  1      1  2012    10.5      14903.
##  2      1  2013    16.5      15375.
##  3      1  2014    10.2      15669.
##  4      1  2015    33.2      16004.
##  5      1  2016     1        15903 
##  6      1  2017     0.995    16543.
##  7      1  2018   147.       15880.
##  8      1  2019     9.63     16720.
##  9      1  2020     0.950    15656.
## 10      1  2021     0.945    17015.
## # ℹ 90 more rows

Creating Categorial Variables

SOEP_final <- SOEP_final %>%
  mutate(
    Sex = factor(Sex),
    Age = as.numeric(Age),
    Federal_State = factor(Federal_State),
    Unemployed = factor(Unemployed),
    Employment_Status = factor(Employment_Status),
    Employment_Level = factor(Employment_Level),
    Yt_1_unemployed = factor(Yt_1_unemployed),
    Party_support = factor(Party_support),
    CCC_Binary = factor(CCC_Binary),
    AFD_Binary = factor(AFD_Binary),
    Industry_Digit_1 = factor(Industry_Digit_1),
    Industry_Digit_2 = factor(Industry_Digit_2),
    ISCO88 = factor(ISCO88),
    ISCO08 = factor(ISCO08), 
    Region = factor(Region)
  )

Checking all variables

### checking all variables 

  for (i in colnames(SOEP_final)) {
  print(i)
  print(class(i))
  print(summary(SOEP_final[, i]))
  }

Descriptive Information

Correlation Table

numeric_columns <- Filter(is.numeric, SOEP_final)

correlation_matrix_AFD<- cor(numeric_columns, as.integer(SOEP_final$AFD_Binary), use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
## Warning in cor(numeric_columns, as.integer(SOEP_final$AFD_Binary), use =
## "pairwise.complete.obs"): the standard deviation is zero
print(correlation_matrix_AFD)
##                                    [,1]
## pid                        0.0095325288
## syear                      0.0697544249
## CCC                        0.0504443206
## Party_strength             0.1385692358
## political_interest        -0.0622947457
## Political_Ideology         0.1394157874

print(correlation_matrix_CCC)

Distributions of selected variables over years

### Climate Change Binary 

ggplot(SOEP_final, aes(x = factor(syear), fill = CCC_Binary)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Binary Variable Across Multiple Years",
       x = "Year",
       y = "AFD") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "blue", "1" = 'black')) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = c(0, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 1))

### AFD Binary 
ggplot(SOEP_final, aes(x = factor(syear), fill = AFD_Binary)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Binary Variable Across Multiple Years",
       x = "Year",
       y = "AFD") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "blue", "1" = 'black')) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = c(0, 0.05, 0.1, 1))

## Political Ideology
ggplot(SOEP_final, aes(x = Political_Ideology, fill = factor(syear))) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
    labs(title = "Difference per year",
         x = 'Political_Ideology',
         y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal()
## Warning: Removed 175722 rows containing non-finite values (`stat_density()`).

## UNEMPLOYED

ggplot(SOEP_final, aes(x = factor(syear), fill = factor(Unemployed))) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Binary Variable Across Multiple Years",
       x = "Year",
       y = "Unemployed") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "blue", "1" = 'black')) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = c(0, 0.05, 0.1, 1))

## Satisfaction_democracy
ggplot(SOEP_final, aes(x = Satisfaction_democracy, fill = factor(syear))) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
    labs(title = "Difference per year",
         x = 'Satisfaction_democracy',
         y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal()+
  xlim(0, 10)
## Warning: Removed 163616 rows containing non-finite values (`stat_density()`).

## Trust_parliament
ggplot(SOEP_final, aes(x = Trust_parliament, fill = factor(syear))) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
    labs(title = "Difference per year",
         x = 'Trust_parliament',
         y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal()+
  xlim(0, 10)
## Warning: Removed 210530 rows containing non-finite values (`stat_density()`).

## political_interest
ggplot(SOEP_final, aes(x = political_interest, fill = factor(syear))) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
    labs(title = "Difference per year",
         x = 'political_interest',
         y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal()+
  xlim(1, 4)
## Warning: Removed 386 rows containing non-finite values (`stat_density()`).

AFD Voters vs Non-AFD VOTERS

ggplot(SOEP_final, aes(x = factor(AFD_Binary), fill = CCC_Binary)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(title = "AFD vs NON AFD",
       x = "Climate Change Binary (1 = NOT WORRIED AT ALL) ",
       y = "Percentage") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "blue", "1" = 'black')) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = c(0, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 1))

### Political interest 

ggplot(SOEP_final, aes(x = political_interest, fill = factor(AFD_Binary))) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
    labs(title = "AFD vs NON_AFD Voters",
         x = 'political_interest',
         y = "Density") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("blue", "black")) +
    theme_minimal()+
  xlim(1, 4)
## Warning: Removed 386 rows containing non-finite values (`stat_density()`).

### Political ideology 

ggplot(SOEP_final, aes(x = Political_Ideology, fill = factor(AFD_Binary))) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
    labs(title = "AFD vs NON_AFD Voters",
         x = 'Political_Ideology',
         y = "Density") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("blue", "black")) +
    theme_minimal()+
  xlim(0, 10)
## Warning: Removed 178235 rows containing non-finite values (`stat_density()`).

### Satisfaction with democracy 

ggplot(SOEP_final, aes(x = Satisfaction_democracy, fill = factor(AFD_Binary))) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
    labs(title = "AFD vs NON_AFD Voters",
         x = 'Satisfaction_democracy',
         y = "Density") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("blue", "black")) +
    theme_minimal() +
  xlim(0, 10)
## Warning: Removed 163616 rows containing non-finite values (`stat_density()`).

### Trust in Parliament 

ggplot(SOEP_final, aes(x = Trust_parliament, fill = factor(AFD_Binary))) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
    labs(title = "AFD vs NON_AFD Voters",
         x = 'Trust_parliament',
         y = "Density") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("blue", "black")) +
    theme_minimal()+
  xlim(0, 10)
## Warning: Removed 210530 rows containing non-finite values (`stat_density()`).

## Years of Education 

ggplot(SOEP_final, aes(x = Education, fill = factor(AFD_Binary))) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
    labs(title = "AFD vs NON_AFD Voters",
         x = 'Education',
         y = "Density") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("blue", "black")) +
    theme_minimal()

### Decile 

ggplot(SOEP_final, aes(x = decile, fill = factor(AFD_Binary))) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
    labs(title = "AFD vs NON_AFD Voters",
         x = 'decile',
         y = "Density") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("blue", "black")) +
    theme_minimal()

#### Income 

ggplot(SOEP_final, aes(x = Real_Income, fill = factor(AFD_Binary))) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "AFD vs NON_AFD Voters",
       x = 'Real_Income',
       y = "Density") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("blue", "black")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  xlim(0, 150000)
## Warning: Removed 3094 rows containing non-finite values (`stat_density()`).

#### Unemployed 

ggplot(SOEP_final, aes(x = factor(AFD_Binary), fill = Unemployed)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(title = "AFD vs NON AFD",
       x = " AFD ",
       y = "Percentag") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "blue", "1" = 'black')) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = c(0, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 1))

CCC for each party

SOEP_final <- SOEP_final %>%
  mutate(Partei = 
           case_when(
    Party_support == 1 ~ 'SPD', # 
    Party_support == 2  ~ 'CDU', #
    Party_support == 3  ~ 'CSU', #
    Party_support == 4  ~ 'FDP', #
    Party_support == 5  ~ 'Gruen', #
    Party_support == 6  ~ 'Die Linke', #
    Party_support == 27  ~ 'AFD', #
    TRUE ~ 'Andere & NA'
           ))

ggplot(SOEP_final, aes(x = factor(Partei), fill = CCC_Binary)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(title = "AFD vs NON AFD",
       x = "Parteien ",
       y = "Percentage") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("0" = "blue", "1" = 'black')) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = c(0, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 1))

SOEP IS

#### INNOVATION SAMPLE 

#SOEP IS Variables of Interest: 
#plh0032 Sorgen Allgemeine Wirtschaftliche Entwicklung
#plh0033 Sorgen eigene Wirtschaftliche Entwicklung
#plh0036 Sorgen Umweltschutz  
#Plh0037 Sorgen Klimawandelfolgen
#plh 0238 Erlfog muss man sich hart erarbeiten 
#plh 0240 Möglichkeit durch soziale Umstände bestimmt 
#plh0137 Jetiges Einkommen gerecht 
#plh0170 Zufriedenheit Demokratie Deutschland 
#Plh0007 Interesse für Politik 
#plh0012 Parteipräferenz 
#plh0013 Parteipräferenz Intesität
#plb0021 Arbeitslos gemeledet
#pspa01 Geldanlagen 
#pspa02 Euro Wert Geldanlage 
### Social Media & Newspaper consumption. 


SOEP_IS_2021 <- read_dta('/Users/finnkruger/Documents/GitHub/Master_thesis_MDS/Data/SOEP/cs-transfer/soep-is.2021_stata_de/p.dta') %>%
  select(pid, syear,
         pfrei19, # Social Media
         pfrei22, # Newspaper
         plh0007 # Interested in politics 
         ) %>%
  rename(
    Social_Media_Frequency = pfrei19,
    Newspaper_Frequency = pfrei22,
    Political_interest_IS = plh0007 
  )

### Hoq many people of SOEP IS are in SOEP CORE ? 
## wie viele observations mit den 9000 IDs

  
### 144 observations from 22 individuals of the innovation sample are also in SOEP CORE

### 10k individuals with 54k observations since 2011 are in more than one year of the SOEP IS
Populist Attiudes fron SOEP-IS
#### Populist Attitudes asked in 2020 from IS 
Populist_Attitudes_2020 <- read_dta('/Users/finnkruger/Documents/GitHub/Master_thesis_MDS/Data/SOEP/cs-transfer/soep-is.2020_stata_de/inno.dta') %>%
  select(pid, syear,
         starts_with('iung')
         )
